spans.rationale: Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a comma, which serves as a syntactic separator without independent assertive content. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a comma, which is punctuation used to separate clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.

The gap text is only the connector 'while' with surrounding spaces. In this sentence it functions to link the two already captured propositions rather than to assert a standalone claim. Although it signals a relationship between clauses, the connector itself is non-assertive and does not warrant separate claim coverage within this frozen gap.

Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a comma, functioning purely as punctuation between clauses. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a comma, which is punctuation used to separate clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.

Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a period, marking the end of the sentence without semantic claim content. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.

The gap text is only the standalone connector "When " at the start of the sentence. It functions as a conditional introducer linking the existing scope claim to the prediction claims, but by itself it does not add a separate claim beyond that connection. Since the substantive conditional content is already present in the captured scope span, this isolated token is best treated as a non-assertive connector rather than a boundary omission candidate.

Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a comma used to separate clauses within the statement. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a comma, which is punctuation used to separate clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.
Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a period terminating the sentence. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.

Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a period marking the end of the sentence. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.

The gap text is only the standalone conditional introducer 'If', which does not itself assert a claim. It functions as a non-assertive connector introducing the falsification conditions already represented by the two existing claims. Since the gap contains only the connector and not omitted substantive condition content, it is best classified as an explicit non-claim.

This is a reason disagreement only, so classification remains explicit_non_claim. The gap text ' or ' is a logical connector expressing disjunction between two adjacent falsification conditions, not a substantive claim on its own. 'List_delimiter' better fits punctuation-like separators in enumerations; here the lexical item 'or' functions primarily as a non-assertive connector.

The gap text ', the pattern is falsified.' is a meta-level consequence stating the overall falsification status if either covered condition occurs. Under atomic claim policy, the substantive falsification conditions are already represented by the two existing claims, and this trailing phrase does not add a separate factual condition or semantic modifier that must be included in a claim boundary.

This is a reason disagreement, so classification remains explicit_non_claim. The gap text ' or ' is a coordinating conjunction expressing alternation between two adjacent claims, not a punctuation-style delimiter. It has no standalone propositional content, so non_assertive_connector is the better fit than list_delimiter.
Reviewer Q: The gap contains only a period ending the sentence. Reviewer G: The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.

Reviewer G: The Gap consists of a comma and the conjunction 'while', which serves as a non-assertive connector contrasting the two adjacent claims without adding any independent claim-bearing content.

This is a reason disagreement only, so classification remains explicit_non_claim. The gap text is exactly comma-plus-space between two already-extracted claims and contains no lexical item or list structure of its own. Although a comma can sometimes function within a list, here the gap is simply punctuation separating the scope phrase from the following proposition, so punctuation_only is the better fit.

This is a reason-only disagreement. The immutable gap span is exactly ', ', which is only punctuation plus whitespace and does not function as a delimiter between list items. It merely punctuates the transition into the limitation clause, so it has no independent claim content.
